title: Lycianthes magdalenae
taxonomy: Lycianthes magdalenae is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family, Solanaceae. It belongs to the genus Lycianthes, which comprises approximately 160 species of shrubs and perennials. Lycianthes magdalenae is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is further classified as a member of the tribe Lycieae within the Solanaceae family. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics, such as its growth habit and flower structure.

intro: Lycianthes magdalenae, also known as the "Magdalena's potato bush," is a perennial shrub native to Central and South America, belonging to the Solanaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lycianthes magdalenae include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as ointments and salves, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
